content: I'm trying to figure out how to budget for my upcoming wedding next June at the Biltmore Estate. David and I are both engineers at SpaceX, but even with our salaries, this is stretching our finances pretty thin.
Congratulations on your engagement! The Biltmore is stunning but definitely a premium venue. Have you created a detailed wedding budget to track all your expenses?

We have a spreadsheet, but it keeps growing. The venue alone is $45,000, and that's not including catering, which will probably be another $30,000 for 150 guests. I've been selling some of my vintage car collection to help cover costs.
That's a significant investment in your special day. Which cars from your collection have you sold so far?

I sold my 1965 Mustang Fastback last month for $32,000, which helped a lot. I'm keeping my 1957 Chevy Bel Air though - that one's too special to me. David thinks I'm crazy for selling cars to pay for a one-day event.
It's a tough balance between creating the wedding of your dreams and maintaining financial stability. Have you considered any alternatives that might be more budget-friendly?

We looked at other venues, but I fell in love with the Biltmore's conservatory for the ceremony. I've been trying to cut costs elsewhere - making my own wedding invitations using my calligraphy skills instead of hiring a stationer.
That's a beautiful way to add a personal touch while saving money. How many invitations are you making?

About 80 invitations. I've already finished 30 of them. I'm also knitting shawls for my bridesmaids instead of buying them gifts. My mom taught me when I was a kid, and it's become a stress-relief hobby for me.
Handmade gifts are so meaningful. Are you incorporating any of your other hobbies into the wedding planning?

Actually, yes! I'm folding 1,000 origami cranes for the reception decor - it's supposed to bring good luck. And for the menu, I'm doing most of the cooking myself, focusing on Thai dishes since that's what David and I love. I just need to figure out how to scale up my recipes for 150 people!
